prompt.title: AgentMind Phase 4: Agent Execution Engine, Pseudoterminal UI & Chat Participant
prompt.description: Build the core orchestration engine — Team Lead logic with 5-phase lifecycle, Teammate agent loop with autonomous task execution, Agent Manager for spawn/monitor/shutdown lifecycle, Task Planner for LLM-powered decomposition, Pseudoterminal UI for per-agent visibility, and the full Chat Participant handler with interactive onboarding. This phase brings the entire system to life.
prerequisites: Phase 1 completed: scaffolding, types, extension entry point
Phase 2 completed: communication infrastructure (taskList, mailbox, lockManager, workspace, agentmindMd)
Phase 3 completed: LLM integration (modelAccess, agentPrompts, toolDefinitions), all tools (file, terminal, communication, code), role presets
All files compile with zero TypeScript errors

instruction:
You are continuing to build AgentMind — a collaborative multi-agent orchestration VS Code extension. Phases 1-3 are complete: scaffolding, types, communication infrastructure, LLM integration, tools, and role presets are all working.

This is Phase 4 of 5. This is the MOST CRITICAL phase — you are building the execution engine that brings everything together. The Team Lead, Teammate agents, Agent Manager, Task Planner, Pseudoterminals, and the full Chat Participant handler all get built here.

BEFORE writing any code, READ these existing files thoroughly:
- `src/types.ts` — All interfaces
- `src/llm/modelAccess.ts` — `AgentLLM` class with `runAgentLoop`
- `src/llm/agentPrompts.ts` — System prompt builders
- `src/llm/toolDefinitions.ts` — Tool definitions and `getToolsForRole()`
- `src/tools/fileTools.ts` — `FileToolHandlers`
- `src/tools/terminalTools.ts` — `TerminalToolHandlers`
- `src/tools/communicationTools.ts` — `CommunicationToolHandlers`, `LeadCommunicationToolHandlers`
- `src/tools/codeTools.ts` — `CodeToolHandlers`
- `src/communication/taskList.ts` — `TaskList`
- `src/communication/mailbox.ts` — `Mailbox`
- `src/communication/lockManager.ts` — `LockManager`
- `src/storage/workspace.ts` — Workspace utilities
- `src/storage/agentmindMd.ts` — Handbook generation
- `src/roles/presets.ts` — Role presets
- `src/extension.ts` — Current entry point (will need updating)

## TASK: Create 7 files and update 2 existing files with COMPLETE implementations.

---

### FILE 1: `src/terminal/formatter.ts`

Implement ANSI terminal formatting utilities for rich pseudoterminal output.

**Requirements:**

Export a `TerminalFormatter` class with static methods:

1. `static header(text: string): string` — Bold white text on blue background, full-width line
2. `static thinking(text: string): string` — Dimmed/italic gray text, prefixed with `💭`
3. `static toolCall(toolName: string, input: string): string` — Cyan text: `🔧 [toolName] input_summary`
4. `static toolResult(success: boolean, output: string): string` — Green (success) or red (failure) text with `✓` or `✗` prefix
5. `static message(from: string, content: string): string` — Yellow text: `💬 [from]: content`
6. `static taskUpdate(taskId: string, status: string): string` — Magenta text: `📋 [taskId] → status`
7. `static error(text: string): string` — Red bold text with `❌` prefix
8. `static success(text: string): string` — Green bold text with `✅` prefix
9. `static info(text: string): string` — White text with `ℹ️` prefix
10. `static separator(): string` — Dimmed horizontal line `─────────────`
11. `static agentHeader(agentId: string, roleName: string, icon: string): string` — Formatted agent identity banner
12. `static progress(completed: number, total: number, label: string): string` — Simple text progress: `[████░░░░] 4/8 label`
13. `static timestamp(): string` — Returns dimmed current time `[HH:MM:SS]`

Use ANSI escape codes:
- `\x1b[1m` bold, `\x1b[2m` dim, `\x1b[3m` italic
- `\x1b[31m` red, `\x1b[32m` green, `\x1b[33m` yellow, `\x1b[34m` blue, `\x1b[35m` magenta, `\x1b[36m` cyan
- `\x1b[0m` reset
- `\x1b[44m` blue background

Truncate long strings to 200 characters with `...` suffix.

---

### FILE 2: `src/terminal/agentTerminal.ts`

Implement the `AgentPseudoterminal` class — one per agent, providing a visible terminal tab.

**Requirements:**

Implement `vscode.Pseudoterminal` interface:

```typescript
class AgentPseudoterminal implements vscode.Pseudoterminal {
  private writeEmitter = new vscode.EventEmitter<string>();
  private closeEmitter = new vscode.EventEmitter<number | void>();
  onDidWrite = this.writeEmitter.event;
  onDidClose = this.closeEmitter.event;
}
```

**Constructor:** `constructor(agentId: string, roleName: string, icon: string)`

**Methods:**

1. `open(initialDimensions?: vscode.TerminalDimensions): void`
   - Write welcome banner using `TerminalFormatter.agentHeader()`
   - Show agent ID, role, and status
   - Write separator line

2. `close(): void` — Clean up, fire close emitter

3. `handleInput(data: string): void`
   - Handle `Ctrl+C` (data === `\x03`): set a `pauseRequested` flag, write pause message
   - Handle other input: ignore (agents are autonomous)

4. `writeLine(text: string): void` — Write text + `\r\n` via writeEmitter
5. `writeThinking(text: string): void` — Write thinking-formatted text
6. `writeToolCall(toolName: string, input: string): void` — Write tool call formatted text
7. `writeToolResult(success: boolean, output: string): void` — Write tool result
8. `writeMessage(from: string, content: string): void` — Write inter-agent message
9. `writeTaskUpdate(taskId: string, status: string): void` — Write task status change
10. `writeError(text: string): void` — Write error message
11. `writeSuccess(text: string): void` — Write success message
12. `writeProgress(completed: number, total: number, label: string): void` — Write progress bar
13. `isPauseRequested(): boolean` — Check if Ctrl+C was pressed (consumed on read)

Also export:
- `createAgentTerminal(agentId: string, roleName: string, icon: string): { terminal: vscode.Terminal, pty: AgentPseudoterminal }`
  - Creates pseudoterminal instance
  - Creates VS Code terminal via `vscode.window.createTerminal({ name: '🤖 AgentMind: {roleName}', pty })`
  - Returns both the terminal (for showing) and the pty (for writing)

---

### FILE 3: `src/orchestrator/taskPlanner.ts`

Implement LLM-powered task decomposition.

**Requirements:**

Export a `TaskPlanner` class.

**Constructor:** `constructor(llm: AgentLLM)`

**Methods:**

1. `async decompose(taskDescription: string, teamRoles: AgentRole[], token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<PlannedTask[]>`

   Where `PlannedTask` is:
   ```typescript
   interface PlannedTask {
     title: string;
     description: string;
     assignedRole: string; // role ID to assign to
     priority: TaskPriority;
     dependencies: number[]; // indices of other planned tasks this depends on
     estimatedComplexity: 'simple' | 'moderate' | 'complex';
   }
   ```

   - Sends a carefully crafted prompt to the LLM asking it to decompose the task
   - The prompt includes: task description, available roles with their expertise, instructions to create specific/actionable subtasks
   - Parses the LLM's JSON response into PlannedTask array
   - Validates: each task has a title, description, valid role assignment
   - Maps dependency indices to actual task IDs after creation
   - Returns the planned tasks

2. `async createTasksFromPlan(plan: PlannedTask[], taskList: TaskList, agentMapping: Map<string, string>): Promise<Task[]>`
   - Converts PlannedTask array into actual Task objects in the shared task list
   - Maps `assignedRole` to actual `agentId` using `agentMapping` (role → agentId)
   - Resolves dependency indices to actual task IDs
   - Creates tasks in dependency order (so IDs exist for deps)
   - Returns created Task objects

3. `async validatePlan(plan: PlannedTask[], teamRoles: AgentRole[]): { valid: boolean, issues: string[] }`
   - Checks: no circular dependencies, all assigned roles exist in team, no duplicate work
   - Checks: at least one task has no dependencies (something can start immediately)
   - Returns validation result

**Prompt engineering:**
- The decomposition prompt must instruct the LLM to:
  - Break the task into 3-15 specific subtasks
  - Assign each to the most appropriate role
  - Set dependencies (task B needs task A's API before implementing UI)
  - Set priorities (critical path items are 'high')
  - Make descriptions detailed enough for an agent to execute independently
  - Output valid JSON array
- Include few-shot example in the prompt showing expected format
- Handle malformed LLM output gracefully (retry once if JSON parsing fails)

---

### FILE 4: `src/orchestrator/teammate.ts`

Implement the `TeammateAgent` class — an autonomous agent that works on assigned tasks.

**Requirements:**

This is the autonomous agent loop. Each teammate is an independent worker.

**Constructor:**
```typescript
constructor(
  agentId: string,
  role: AgentRole,
  model: vscode.LanguageModelChat,
  taskList: TaskList,
  mailbox: Mailbox,
  workspaceRoot: string,
  pty: AgentPseudoterminal,
  agentmindMdContent: string
)
```

**Properties:**
- `private active: boolean = true`
- `private currentTaskId: string | null = null`
- `private llm: AgentLLM`
- `private toolHandlers: Map<string, (input: unknown) => Promise<ToolCallResult>>`
- `private state: AgentState`

**Methods:**

1. `async start(token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<void>`
   - THE MAIN AGENT LOOP. Runs until `active === false` or token is cancelled.
   - Initializes LLM with role-specific system prompt + AGENTMIND.md context
   - Registers all tool handlers for this agent's role
   - Loop:
     ```
     while (this.active && !token.isCancellationRequested) {
       try {
         // 1. Write heartbeat to state file
         await this.writeHeartbeat();

         // 2. Check for pause request from terminal
         if (this.pty.isPauseRequested()) {
           this.pty.writeInfo('Agent paused. Waiting...');
           await this.delay(5000);
           continue;
         }

         // 3. Check inbox for messages
         const messages = await this.checkAndProcessMessages();

         // 4. If no current task, try to pick one up
         if (!this.currentTaskId) {
           const task = await this.pickNextTask();
           if (!task) {
             this.pty.writeThinking('No tasks available. Waiting...');
             await this.delay(pollingInterval);
             continue;
           }
           this.currentTaskId = task.id;
         }

         // 5. Work on current task
         await this.workOnTask(this.currentTaskId);

       } catch (error) {
         this.pty.writeError(`Error: ${error}`);
         await this.delay(5000); // Back off on error
       }
     }
     ```
   - On exit: update state to 'shutdown', write final heartbeat

2. `private async checkAndProcessMessages(): Promise<AgentMessage[]>`
   - Gets unread messages from mailbox
   - Displays each in the terminal
   - If message contains task-relevant info, injects it into LLM context
   - Returns processed messages

3. `private async pickNextTask(): Promise<Task | null>`
   - Gets available tasks from task list
   - Filters to tasks assigned to this agent, or unassigned tasks matching this role's expertise
   - Claims the highest-priority matching task
   - Writes task update to terminal
   - Returns claimed task or null

4. `private async workOnTask(taskId: string): Promise<void>`
   - Gets task details from task list
   - Builds a work prompt: 'You are working on task: {title}\n{description}\nFiles in the workspace: ...\nPrevious messages: ...'
   - Updates task status to 'in-progress'
   - Calls `llm.runAgentLoop()` with:
     - The work prompt
     - Tool handlers for this role
     - `onStream`: pipes text to `pty.writeThinking()`
     - `onToolCall`: pipes to `pty.writeToolCall()`
   - When LLM finishes (no more tool calls), extracts result summary
   - Marks task as completed with result and files modified
   - Sets `this.currentTaskId = null`
   - Writes success message to terminal

5. `private async writeHeartbeat(): Promise<void>`
   - Updates state file with current timestamp, status, current task

6. `private registerToolHandlers(): Map<string, (input: unknown) => Promise<ToolCallResult>>`
   - Creates instances of FileToolHandlers, TerminalToolHandlers, CommunicationToolHandlers, CodeToolHandlers
   - Maps tool names to their handler methods
   - Only includes tools allowed for this agent's role

7. `async shutdown(reason: string): Promise<void>`
   - Sets `active = false`
   - If working on a task, saves progress and sets task back to 'pending'
   - Writes shutdown message to terminal
   - Updates state to 'shutdown'

8. `getState(): AgentState` — Returns current agent state

**Helper:**
- `private delay(ms: number): Promise<void>` — Promise-based delay for polling

---

### FILE 5: `src/orchestrator/agentManager.ts`

Implement the Agent Manager — spawns, monitors, and shuts down teammate agents.

**Requirements:**

**Constructor:**
```typescript
constructor(
  model: vscode.LanguageModelChat,
  taskList: TaskList,
  mailbox: Mailbox,
  lockManager: LockManager,
  workspaceRoot: string,
  agentmindMdContent: string
)
```

**Properties:**
- `private agents: Map<string, { agent: TeammateAgent, terminal: vscode.Terminal, pty: AgentPseudoterminal }>`
- `private active: boolean = true`

**Methods:**

1. `async spawnTeammate(agentId: string, role: AgentRole, token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<TeammateAgent>`
   - Creates an `AgentPseudoterminal` via `createAgentTerminal()`
   - Shows the terminal tab
   - Creates a `TeammateAgent` instance
   - Stores in agents map
   - Starts the agent loop in a detached async context (fire-and-forget with error handling)
   - Logs spawn event
   - Returns the agent

2. `async spawnAll(teamConfig: TeamAgentConfig[], token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<void>`
   - Spawns all teammates from the team config
   - Spawns sequentially with short delay between each (prevent terminal tab flood)
   - Logs total team size

3. `async shutdownTeammate(agentId: string, reason: string): Promise<void>`
   - Calls agent.shutdown(reason)
   - Disposes terminal
   - Removes from agents map

4. `async shutdownAll(reason: string): Promise<void>`
   - Shuts down all agents
   - Waits for all shutdowns to complete
   - Sets active = false

5. `getTeamStatus(): { agentId: string, role: string, status: AgentStatus, currentTask: string | null }[]`
   - Returns status of all agents

6. `async monitorHealth(): Promise<{ agentId: string, issue: string }[]>`
   - Checks heartbeats for all agents
   - Returns list of agents with issues (stale heartbeat, error state)

7. `isActive(): boolean` — Returns whether the manager is still running

8. `getAgent(agentId: string): TeammateAgent | undefined`

---

### FILE 6: `src/orchestrator/teamLead.ts`

Implement the Team Lead — the central orchestrator that manages the entire lifecycle.

**Requirements:**

This is the BRAIN of AgentMind. The lead runs in the chat participant context and drives the entire workflow.

**Constructor:**
```typescript
constructor(
  model: vscode.LanguageModelChat,
  taskList: TaskList,
  mailbox: Mailbox,
  agentManager: AgentManager,
  workspaceRoot: string,
  stream: vscode.ChatResponseStream
)
```

**Main method:**

1. `async execute(teamConfig: TeamConfig, token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<void>`
   - THE FULL LIFECYCLE. Runs through all 5 phases, only exits when ALL succeed or user cancels.

   **Phase 1: PLANNING**
   - Stream to chat: `## 📋 Phase 1: Planning\nDecomposing your task into subtasks...`
   - Use `TaskPlanner.decompose()` to break the task into subtasks
   - Validate the plan
   - If `autoApproveTaskPlan` is false, show the plan in chat and wait for confirmation (via follow-up)
   - Stream the task plan as a formatted table
   - Update team status to 'planning'

   **Phase 2: ASSIGNING**
   - Stream: `## 🎯 Phase 2: Assigning Tasks`
   - Create tasks in the shared task list via `TaskPlanner.createTasksFromPlan()`
   - Each task gets assigned to the appropriate agent
   - Send notification messages to each agent about their assignments
   - Stream assignment summary to chat

   **Phase 3: MONITORING (the main loop)**
   - Stream: `## 🔄 Phase 3: Execution & Monitoring`
   - Run a monitoring loop:
     ```
     while (hasPendingTasks && !token.isCancellationRequested) {
       // Check task progress
       const summary = await taskList.getTaskSummary();
       
       // Stream progress update to chat periodically
       stream.markdown(`Progress: ${summary.completed}/${summary.total} tasks complete`);
       
       // Check agent health
       const healthIssues = await agentManager.monitorHealth();
       for (const issue of healthIssues) {
         // Handle: respawn crashed agent, reassign stuck task
       }
       
       // Check for messages to lead
       const messages = await mailbox.getUnreadMessages('lead');
       // Process: respond to blocker reports, resolve conflicts
       
       // Check for duplicate work (two agents modifying same files)
       await this.detectDuplicateWork();
       
       // Wait before next check
       await delay(pollingInterval);
     }
     ```
   - When all tasks complete, exit monitoring loop

   **Phase 4: VERIFICATION**
   - Stream: `## ✅ Phase 4: Verification`
   - Use LLM to generate and run verification commands (e.g., `npm test`, `npx tsc --noEmit`)
   - Check diagnostics on modified files
   - Collect and analyze results
   - If issues found → create fix tasks → go back to Phase 3 (MONITORING)
   - Maximum 3 fix iterations to prevent infinite loops

   **Phase 5: COMPLETION**
   - Stream: `## 🎉 Phase 5: Complete!`
   - Generate final summary:
     - Tasks completed: X/Y
     - Files created/modified: list
     - Agents used: list with work summaries
     - Test results (if any)
   - Shut down all agents via AgentManager
   - Update team status to 'completed'
   - Stream the final report to chat

**Helper methods:**

2. `private async detectDuplicateWork(): Promise<void>`
   - Gets all in-progress tasks
   - Checks if any two tasks are modifying the same files
   - If detected: send message to the later-starting agent to coordinate

3. `private async handleBlockerReport(message: AgentMessage): Promise<void>`
   - Analyzes the blocker
   - If it's a dependency issue: check if blocking task is done
   - If it's a technical issue: use LLM to suggest a resolution, send back to agent

4. `private async runVerificationCommands(filesModified: string[]): Promise<{ passed: boolean, output: string }>`
   - Runs relevant commands based on project type (npm test, tsc, eslint, etc.)
   - Returns aggregated results

---

### FILE 7: `src/participant/onboarding.ts`

Implement the interactive team configuration flow within Copilot Chat.

**Requirements:**

Export an `OnboardingFlow` class that manages the multi-step team setup.

**State machine approach:**
```
START → ASK_TEAM_SIZE → ASK_ROLES(for each agent) → CONFIRM → CREATE_TEAM
```

**Constructor:** `constructor(stream: vscode.ChatResponseStream)`

**Methods:**

1. `async run(taskDescription: string, context: vscode.ChatContext, token: vscode.CancellationToken): Promise<TeamConfig | null>`
   - Manages the full onboarding flow
   - Uses `context.history` to detect which step we're in (by checking previous assistant messages and user responses)
   - Returns the completed TeamConfig or null if cancelled

2. `presentTeamSizeQuestion(stream: vscode.ChatResponseStream): void`
   - Streams markdown asking how many agents
   - Uses `stream.button()` to present options: 2, 3, 4, 5
   - Each button has a `vscode.ChatFollowup` with `prompt` like 'team-size:3'

3. `presentRoleQuestion(stream: vscode.ChatResponseStream, agentIndex: number, totalAgents: number): void`
   - Streams: `### Agent ${agentIndex + 1} of ${totalAgents}\nChoose a role:`
   - Presents buttons for each role preset (icon + name)
   - Plus: 'Let Lead Decide' button and 'Custom Role' button
   - Button prompts like 'role:1:backend-dev', 'role:1:auto', 'role:1:custom'

4. `presentConfirmation(stream: vscode.ChatResponseStream, config: TeamConfig): void`
   - Shows a summary table of the planned team
   - Button: 'Start Team' and 'Reconfigure'

5. `parseUserResponse(prompt: string): { type: 'team-size' | 'role' | 'confirm' | 'custom-role' | 'unknown', value: string, index?: number }`
   - Parses button follow-up prompts to extract user selections

6. `buildTeamConfig(taskDescription: string, teamSize: number, roleSelections: Map<number, string>): TeamConfig`
   - Constructs the final TeamConfig from user's selections
   - For 'auto' roles, assigns a placeholder (lead will decide during planning)
   - Generates agent IDs

**Important:**
- The onboarding flow must work WITHIN the Copilot Chat turn system — each user response creates a new turn
- Use `request.prompt` to detect follow-up responses (check for 'team-size:', 'role:', etc.)
- Store intermediate state in extension context globalState between turns
- Handle edge cases: user types freeform instead of clicking buttons → parse intelligently

---

### UPDATE existing FILE: `src/participant/handler.ts`

Replace the stub with the FULL implementation.

**Requirements:**

The handler is the entry point for everything. It must:

1. On FIRST invocation (no team active):
   - Parse task from `request.prompt`
   - If prompt starts with 'team-size:', 'role:', or 'confirm:' → handle as onboarding response
   - Otherwise → start fresh onboarding: display welcome message, begin team size question

2. On subsequent invocations (during onboarding):
   - Continue the onboarding flow based on stored state
   - When onboarding completes → initialize workspace → generate AGENTMIND.md → create TeamLead → spawn agents → start execution

3. On invocations during team execution:
   - `/status` command → show team status dashboard
   - `/stop` command → gracefully shut down all agents
   - `/plan` command → show current task plan
   - Regular messages → forward to lead agent as new instructions

4. Follow-up provider:
   - Provides contextual follow-up buttons based on current state
   - During onboarding: team size buttons, role buttons
   - During execution: status, stop
   - After completion: review, restart

**Full initialization sequence (after onboarding):**
```typescript
// 1. Get workspace root
const workspaceRoot = vscode.workspace.workspaceFolders?.[0]?.uri.fsPath;

// 2. Initialize .agentmind/ directory
await initializeWorkspace(workspaceRoot);

// 3. Select LLM model
const model = await AgentLLM.selectModel();

// 4. Create shared services
const lockManager = new LockManager(workspaceRoot);
const taskList = new TaskList(workspaceRoot, lockManager);
const mailbox = new Mailbox(workspaceRoot, lockManager);

// 5. Generate and write AGENTMIND.md
const conventions = await detectProjectConventions(workspaceRoot);
const fileOverview = await getWorkspaceFileOverview(workspaceRoot);
const mdContent = await generateAgentmindMd({ ... });
await writeAgentmindMd(workspaceRoot, mdContent);

// 6. Create Agent Manager
const agentManager = new AgentManager(model, taskList, mailbox, lockManager, workspaceRoot, mdContent);

// 7. Spawn all teammate agents
await agentManager.spawnAll(teamConfig.agents, token);

// 8. Create and run Team Lead
const teamLead = new TeamLead(model, taskList, mailbox, agentManager, workspaceRoot, stream);
await teamLead.execute(teamConfig, token);
```

---

### UPDATE existing FILE: `src/extension.ts`

Update to properly wire the handler and support the full lifecycle.

**Changes:**
- Import the real handler (no longer stub)
- Register disposables for cleanup
- Add a command registration for manual stop: `agentmind.stopTeam`
- Store active team reference for the stop command
- Ensure `deactivate()` triggers agent shutdown

---

## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

After creating/updating all files:
1. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — zero TypeScript errors
2. Run `npm run compile` — esbuild bundles successfully
3. Verify the following flows compile correctly:
   - Handler receives request → starts onboarding → collects team config
   - Handler initializes workspace → spawns agents → starts lead
   - TeamLead runs 5-phase lifecycle
   - TeammateAgent runs autonomous loop with tool calling
   - AgentManager monitors health and handles shutdowns
4. Verify pseudoterminal output formatting looks correct (test TerminalFormatter methods)
5. Verify TaskPlanner generates valid JSON from LLM responses

## IMPORTANT RULES
- This is the INTEGRATION phase — everything must connect correctly
- The TeammateAgent.workOnTask() method calls AgentLLM.runAgentLoop() — this is the critical path
- The TeamLead.execute() method drives the entire lifecycle — it must be robust
- Handle CancellationToken throughout — user can cancel at any point
- Fire-and-forget async operations MUST have error handlers (no unhandled rejections)
- The onboarding flow must work within VS Code's chat turn model — state persists via globalState
- All terminal output must use TerminalFormatter for consistent formatting
- The monitoring loop must not spin — use appropriate polling intervals
- Error recovery: if an agent crashes, the lead should detect and handle it
- The lead agent must ACTUALLY use the LLM to make decisions (not hardcoded logic)
- Keep the team running until the lead explicitly completes or user cancels
success_criteria: All 7 new files created and 2 files updated with complete implementations
TeammateAgent runs autonomous loop: check inbox → pick task → work (LLM + tools) → complete → repeat
TeamLead executes 5-phase lifecycle: plan → assign → monitor → verify → complete
TaskPlanner decomposes tasks via LLM and creates them in the task list
Pseudoterminal shows rich formatted output per agent
Chat participant handler manages full onboarding flow with buttons
Onboarding persists state across chat turns via globalState
CancellationToken is respected throughout the entire system
Agent errors are caught and reported, not silently swallowed
Lead monitoring loop detects stuck/crashed agents
Duplicate work detection works by checking overlapping file modifications
